product.one_line_description: The WhatsApp Business Platform enables medium and large businesses to communicate with their customers at scale through an API.

authentication.auth_summary: Authentication to the WhatsApp Business Platform Cloud API requires an access token (either a System User access token or a User access token) generated with the `whatsapp_business_management` permission. This token must be included in the `Authorization` HTTP header of requests as a Bearer Token.

developer_access.access_notes: To use the WhatsApp Business Platform, a business needs to be verified. Developers must set up a Meta Business Account and a WhatsApp Business Account through Business Manager. While temporary access tokens are available for testing, permanent access tokens require a System User created in Business Manager, implying administrative oversight and approval for full production access.

api_surface.api_summary: The WhatsApp Business Platform provides a public API composed of a Cloud API and an On-Premises API. It is a RESTful API with extensive capabilities for sending and receiving messages, managing business profiles, handling media, and setting up webhooks for real-time notifications. The API supports various messaging features and business operations.

mcp.mcp_summary: No information regarding Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers or related functionalities was found in the provided documentation.

buildability.primary_blocker: Requires vendor/support approval for admin credentials.
buildability.verdict_reasoning: [PROVISIONAL V1] Admin gated access explicitly requires external vendor authorization.

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: After you've finished testing and are ready to go live, you can generate a permanent access token with the System User that you created in Business Manager.

evidence_pool.evidence_snippet: Set up webhooks to receive important notifications including when a customer sends a message to your business.
